nodes.name: Daily 10AM IST
nodes.notes: Runs daily at 10:00. Workflow timezone is set to Asia/Kolkata in Settings, so this fires at 10:00 IST.

nodes.name: Fetch Overdue Invoices
nodes.notes: GET /api/invoices/overdue (internal auth). Returns { invoices: [...], meta } of overdue, still-unpaid invoices ACROSS ALL ORGS. The endpoint already computes days_overdue, the reminder tier + template_name, the template_params, and excludes invoices reminded today (IST) and those with no customer phone.

nodes.name: Split Into Invoices
nodes.notes: Fans the invoices array out to one item per overdue invoice for per-invoice processing. This is the last node whose OUTPUT carries full invoice fields and is 1:1 with the source (no filtering), so downstream nodes reference it by name for item-stable lineage.

nodes.name: Not Reminded Today
nodes.notes: Defense-in-depth guard for the 'max 1 reminder per invoice per day' rule. The endpoint already filters these out; this ensures it even if include_reminded=true is ever passed. Tiering itself is computed server-side and arrives on each item as reminder_tier + template_name.

nodes.name: Send WhatsApp Reminder

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ to: $json.customer_phone, type: 'template', orgId: $json.organization_id, template: { name: $json.template_name, language: { code: $json.language_code }, components: [ { type: 'body', parameters: $json.template_params.map(p => ({ type: 'text', text: p })) } ] } }) }}

nodes.notes: POST /api/whatsapp/send (internal auth) → Meta Cloud API. Builds the tier-specific payment_reminder template body. template_params order: customer, invoice number, amount, days overdue ({{1}}..{{4}}).

nodes.name: Record Reminder Sent

parameters.url: ={{ /* invoice id from Split (not the Filter): Split is the last 1:1, un-filtered output, so its paired-item lineage stays index-stable; the Filter drops items and is a fragile ref target */ $env.APP_URL }}/api/invoices/{{ $('Split Into Invoices').item.json.id }}/reminder
parameters.jsonBody: ={{ /* message_id comes from the immediately-preceding Send response ($json); the HTTP node strips invoice fields, so reminder_tier is read from Split, the last node that still carried it. Route derives org from the invoice PK — no orgId sent. */ JSON.stringify({ message_id: $json.messageId, reminder_tier: $('Split Into Invoices').item.json.reminder_tier }) }}

nodes.notes: POST /api/invoices/[id]/reminder (internal auth). Stamps last_reminder_at = now() and bumps reminder_count so the same invoice is not reminded again today. The Send node returns only { sent, messageId }, so invoice fields (id, reminder_tier) are read from $('Split Into Invoices').item — Split is the last un-filtered 1:1 node, giving stable paired-item lineage; messageId is read from the immediately-preceding Send response.

nodes.name: Log Reminder Activity

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ /* the preceding Record node returns only { data: { reminder_count... } }, so invoice fields are read from Split — the last un-filtered 1:1 node — for index-stable paired-item lineage (the Filter drops items and is a fragile ref target) */ JSON.stringify({ orgId: $('Split Into Invoices').item.json.organization_id, chatPhone: $('Split Into Invoices').item.json.customer_phone, messageType: 'payment_reminder_sent', intent: $('Split Into Invoices').item.json.reminder_tier, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() }) }}

nodes.notes: POST /api/analytics/log-intent (internal auth) → PostHog. Privacy-safe: orgId + masked phone + tier only, never amounts or raw content (CLAUDE.md security rule #8). Reads invoice fields from $('Split Into Invoices').item (last un-filtered 1:1 node), not the Record node's response which strips them.

nodes.name: Error Trigger
nodes.notes: Set this workflow as its own Error Workflow in Settings > Error Workflow.

nodes.name: Log Error

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ source: 'n8n_payment_reminder', error: $json.error?.message, workflow: $json.workflow?.name, node: $json.execution?.lastNodeExecuted, executionId: $json.execution?.id, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() }) }}

nodes.notes: Logs failures to Sentry via /api/errors/log. Never messages a customer chat.
